name: Larson v. Domestic & Foreign Commerce Corp.
summary: Summarized the Court's jurisprudence on sovereign immunity and officer suits, holding that when a government officer acts within valid statutory authority, those actions are attributed to the government, but the officer remains personally liable under general tort law.
significance: Respondents cited it as the Court's clearest summary of the principle that government agents are liable for their own torts under ordinary agency law, even when acting for the government, undermining the claim that Yearsley creates a special immunity.
